Tested Attack Vector: Auctioning a card actively assigned to the Gold Mine
Explanation: Server automatically removes the card from the assigned building array when escrowed.

Tested Attack Vector: Selling an active escrowed card while offline
Explanation: Ownership transfer is atomic and correctly removes the card from the seller's inventory upon next load.

Tested Attack Vector: Injecting future client timestamps to bypass daily cooldowns
Explanation: Server utilizes authoritative backend clock to evaluate daily reset periods.

Tested Attack Vector: Submitting a valid sacrifice alongside an invalid one to trigger a mid-flight crash
Explanation: Database transaction rolls back atomically. Valid items are not destroyed.

Tested Attack Vector: Executing delayed combat hashes after logging out and reconnecting
Explanation: Progression counter (q) is persistently validated; old hashes trigger Error 118 desyncs.
